widgets_values:
Download comfyui_portrait_lora64.safetensors and place in /models/loras/
https://huggingface.co/ali-vilab/ACE_Plus/tree/main/portrait

Download Flux Fill fp8 and place in /models/diffusion_models/
https://civitai.com/models/969431/flux-fill-fp8

OR Flux Fill fp16 and place in /models/diffusion_models/
https://huggingface.co/black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-Fill-dev
This version is more demanding for your hardware

Download Flux Turbo Lora and place in /models/loras/
https://civitai.com/models/876388/flux1-turbo-alpha

clip_l, t5xxl_fp16 (or t5xxl_fp8) can be downloaded from the model manager

OPTIONAL FOR FUTURE
Download comfyui_subject_lora16.safetensors and place in /models/loras/
https://huggingface.co/ali-vilab/ACE_Plus/tree/main/subject

Download comfyui_local_lora16.safetensors and place in /models/loras/
https://huggingface.co/ali-vilab/ACE_Plus/tree/main/local_editing

SUPER FAST - 8s on 4090
TeaCache ON
Sampler: Euler - 12 steps
Turbo Lora


FAST - 15s on 4090
TeaCache ON
Sampler: Euler - 25 steps



BEST QUALITY - ~40s on 4090
TeaCache OFF
Sampler: Euler Ancestral - 25 steps

This update on the workflow adds cropping and stitching to improve quality even in faces that are further away. 
Increase context_expand_pixels to get more area around your face or decrease to crop in closer.

Regulate light intensity here.
MAX = 1 is max light intensity.
Multiplier's best values (in my testings) are between 0.15 and 0.21

Use iclight_sd15_fc model for no backgrounds workflows,
use the fbc model for background workflows.
Models found here: https://huggingface.co/lllyasviel/ic-light/tree/main

Optional Load Image node to load the subject directly instead of generating it.
Connect it to the image resize node input.
This Group blends the original image on top of the new background

If you wish to relight the image by using a mask, you can either create a mask here using the open in mask editor option, or by using a pre-existing mask in the relight Group. Connect the appropriate mask output.

You can open the Load Image node in Mask editor, create a mask and plug the mask output from the Preview Bridge node in the Blend original subject Group, or plug in a Load Image as mask node here as well if you don't want to create a mask via Photoshop or other tools.
product photography, still life photography of a whiskey bottle, white backdrop

Generate High Frequency composite layer, with details from the original image blended into the high frequency layer of the relit image based on the masked area of the preview bridge node of the relit image

Mask the details area you wish to retain from the original image in the Preview Bridge node up here, or use the mask from the segment anything nodes

The mask goes into the Blend Image by mask node in the Blend High Frequency Layers subgroup

Thanks to the amazing u/Powered_JJ on Reddit, whom I have to thank for being open to collaborate with me on this, we now have a native Frequency Separation workflow inside of comfyUI.

This group eliminates the need for either color correcting and blending the detail area, or using Frequency Separation in Photoshop.
Simply mask the detail area you'd like to keep from the original image, and the relit image will be re-processed with a high frequency layer that is the blended result of the high frequency layer of the original image (for the masked area) and the high frequency layer of the generated image (for the areas that you don't care about having different details). 

Once again, thanks u/Powered_JJ

This group is for those of you who need multiple SAM in their life in order to make things work.

Not judging. Just plug in two SAM masks here, and create more MaskComposites to add even more SAMs.
Then use the composited mask however you want, like for keeping colors and / or details over multiple subjects.
IPAdapter group to either act on the whole image with a reference image, or act on just one part of the image by using an attention mask created by a SAM.

Mask the details area you wish to retain from the original image in the Preview Bridge node from the relight group, or use the mask from the segment anything nodes

The mask goes into the Blend Image by mask node in the Blend High Frequency Layers subgroup

This Group generates a product shot.
Connect the resulting generated product image to the depth anything node and to the groundingdinoSAMsegment node.

Model Group.
Remember, SD1.5 only.

Someone asked for PAG as well, so there you go. Disabled by default, remember to set the CFG accordingly, since PAG affects CFG values.
Blended image with finer details from the original image and new lighting from the generated image

Option 1 for color matching the subject and preserving its original color, the number 1 complaint y'all had.

It uses a reduced mask from SAM to blend the original color with the generated scene using a ColorMatch node. Fix the blending value however much you like, personally in my photography works I never go full original color because it makes no sense - but you do you.

Option 2 for color matching the subject and preserving its original color, the number 1 complaint y'all had.

It uses a reduced mask from SAM to blend the original color with the generated scene using a Image Blending Mode node set on Hue. Fix the blending value however much you like, personally in my photography works I never go full original color because it makes no sense - but you do you.
Unfortunately Hue retains some of the original lighting information, so an effective 100% blending turns back the lighting to the original image.
Mask (either from SAM's convert mask to image or from the above Convert Mask to Image node descending from the preview bridge node of the relight group) goes here

This Group recalculates the Low Frequency Layer much in the same way we're doing for the High Frequency Layer, by Color Matching the whole relit image with the Original Image as reference, and then masking with SAM to recolor only the subject.

It then regenerates a new blended low frequency layer.

Quick and dirty SUPIR upscale to 1.2x (it's faster in testing than 2x).
You can swap this for any upscaler you like the most, as long as you understand what's going on.

IMPORTANT: the original image and the SAM mask(s) MUST be resized to the same factor, otherwise the frequency separation technique won't work. I converted the factor to an input to help with that.

MORE IMPORTANT: upscaling the high frequency layer from the original image is not great. Starting from a higher resolution original image and using that as source would be better, but I can't implement it as a one-click solution.

This Group recalculates the Low Frequency Layer much in the same way we're doing for the High Frequency Layer, by Color Matching the whole relit image with the Original Image as reference, and then masking with SAM to recolor only the subject.

It then regenerates a new blended low frequency layer.

Option 3 for color matching the subject and preserving its original color, the number 1 complaint y'all had.

A highly experimental and non-deterministic approach to color matching, this node color matches based on light and shadows, resulting in good-ish pictures if the source is not too different from the relit image, and in weird results if the source is radically different from the relit image.
For those of you who like to experiment in life.

This Group recalculates the Low Frequency Layer much in the same way we're doing for the High Frequency Layer, by Color Matching the whole relit image with the Original Image as reference, and then masking with SAM to recolor only the subject.

It then regenerates a new blended low frequency layer.
You can open import a Mask to use as a light source here, and link it to the Grow Mask With Blur node.

You can open use the Color to Mask node to simulate a "HDRI" (veeery loose term here) by turning the lighter areas of the re-background image into a light mask.
Connect it to the Grow Mask with Blur node.
You can draw your mask here by opening the image in the Mask Editor, and plugging the mask output in the grow mask with blur node.

This Group generates a new background, while maintaining the object coordinates in the same position through the Depth ControlNet.
The original object is then merged on top of this layer in the next group

This Group segments the original object from the background. 
Change the prompt in the GroundingDinoSAMSegment node to the appropriate description of the object
Generate Frequency Separation layers for relit image
Generate Frequency Separation layers for original image

Fun Group to create attention masks for the IPAdapter group, so that only one thing gets affected by it.

In my example, I turned a prop wooden branch into wool, while keeping everything else unaffected by the IPAdapter group.

Golden-hour beach scene with soft waves lapping against the shore, a warm orange-pink sky reflecting on wet sand, scattered seashells and footprints, palm trees swaying gently in the breeze—ideal romantic lighting for a silhouette or lifestyle shoot.

Urban city street background with sunlit sidewalks, modern glass buildings, scattered pedestrians, and light traffic; clear blue sky overhead, casting crisp shadows—perfect natural noon lighting for a fashion model's pose

nodes.type: If ANY execute A else B

properties.Node name for S&R: If ANY execute A else B

widgets_values:
This image depicts a spacious, elegantly designed room with high ceilings and intricate architectural details. The room features a herringbone-patterned wooden floor that extends throughout the space, adding a touch of sophistication. The walls are painted white, enhancing the room's brightness and making the space feel larger and more open.

On the left side of the room, there is a large window with a grid pattern, allowing natural light to flood the area. Adjacent to this window is a smaller window with an arched top, which also contributes to the room's airy and airy atmosphere.

In the center of the room, there is a modern, minimalist chair with a black seat and backrest, placed on a small, modern side table. The table has a sleek, metallic finish and holds a vase with a few flowers, adding a touch of greenery and color to the room.

The room also features tall, arched doorways with glass panels, which likely lead to other rooms or areas within the house. The doors are framed with ornate molding, matching the room's overall classical and elegant style.

Overall, the room exudes a sense of luxury and sophistication, with its high ceilings, intricate moldings, and carefully chosen furnishings.

widgets_values: realistic face swap, retain original face shape, size, and position, natural blend with body, no distortion, no overexposure, adapt face skin color to neck skin color

(Optional) Preserve details, such as words, from the original image
Mask (if you decide to use a custom one from the Preview Bridge node above)
Mask (if you decide to use the mask from SAM))

Blend Low Frequency Layers for Color Match
